description_long: This watermill features a long, rectangular building with a pitched, tiled roof in a reddish-brown hue. The structure is primarily constructed from white plaster or stone walls, supported by brick or timber columns at the ends. At one end of the building, a large wooden water wheel is mounted, partially enclosed by a small open shed with visible support beams. A narrow, dark mill race runs beneath the wheel, channeling water to power it. The overall design is rustic and functional, with no visible windows and a focus on the water wheel mechanism.
description: A rustic watermill with a tiled gabled roof, white walls, and a large wooden water wheel powered by a narrow mill race.
description_view_0: Front view showing the water wheel, mill race, and open shed structure at the end of the building.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the building, the tiled roof, and the water wheel partially enclosed by the shed.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain white wall and the full expanse of the tiled roof, with no visible openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the water wheel, mill race, and the building's side wall.
attribute_water_wheel: Large undershot wooden water wheel, approximately 2.2 meters in diameter, constructed from dark timber with visible paddles and a central hub; features a simple, robust design with no visible spokes or decorative elements.
attribute_mill_race: Present as a narrow, open channel running beneath the wheel, constructed from dark stone or concrete, approximately 0.5 meters wide and 6 meters long, with a straight, utilitarian form.
attribute_building_structure: Single-story rectangular building with white plaster or stone walls, supported by brick or timber columns; foundation not clearly visible; features a small open shed at one end for the water wheel.
attribute_roof_type: Gabled roof with reddish-brown tiles, no visible dormers, chimneys, or eaves; simple and functional design.
